techmeme-2022.text: Amazon is working on letting Alexa mimic any voice after hearing less than a minute of audio, as a way to “make the memories last” of deceased family members
Amazon's Latest Trick: Pipe In a Dead Person's Voice Through Alexa's Speakers
Alexa will soon be able to read stories as your dead grandma
Alexa has a new voice — your dead relative's
Amazon shows off Alexa feature that mimics the voices of your dead relatives
Amazon's Alexa could turn dead loved ones' voices into digital assistant

Amazon's Alexa will soon be able to read you stories in a loved one's voice - even if they're dead
Amazon demonstrates Alexa mimicking the voice of a deceased relative
Amazon's new pitch: let Alexa speak as your relatives from beyond the grave
Alexa's head scientist on conversational exploration, ambient AI
Amazon demos Alexa reading a bedtime story in the voice of a boy's deceased grandma

Here's one Alexa feature we hope the HomePod never gets
Amazon to give Alexa the ability to sound like anyone
Alexa Wants To Resurrect Your Loved Ones By Using Their Voice
Amazon's Alexa has become a “Black Mirror” writing prompt
Amazon feature has Alexa speaking in voice of late relative
Amazon's Alexa May Soon Be Able to Mimic Your Dead Relatives' Voices
Alexa will soon be able to mimic the voices of anyone alive or dead
Amazon shows off Alexa's new in-development ability to mimic anyone dead or alive
Amazon Reveals ‘Voice Replication’ Feature For Alexa To Mimic Loved Ones

Amazon's Alexa voice assistant will be able to mimic any person's voice after hearing a short audio clip. Amazon markets the tech as a way to remember a deceased family member. https://gizmodo.com/...

The phone attacking implications of this tool are not good at all — this will likely be used for impersonation. At Amazon's re:MARS conference they announced they're working to use short audio clips of a person's voice & reprogram it for longer speech https://gizmodo.com/...

Amazon knows full well that this idea is incredibly creepy and absolutely nobody will do it. What people will actually do is make Alexa sound like Beyoncé, but they'd be in litigation tout de suite if they admitted that's what it's for. https://twitter.com/...
Meanwhile, in the imminent dystopia category https://www.reuters.com/...
I miss my parents deeply, but the idea of interacting with their voices to turn on lights or change the TV volume strikes me as not just surreal but upsetting. https://www.cnbc.com/...
“Hey.... So I was watching Black Mirror the other day and it gave me this awesome product idea!” https://twitter.com/...
This will, of course, not be abused in any way or backfire at all. I see no downside. https://twitter.com/...

Amazon keeping it creepy with deep fake ghosts: “In the scenario presented at the event, the voice of a deceased loved one (a grandmother, in this case), is used to read a grandson a bedtime story” https://techcrunch.com/...

I'm absolutely amazed this idea went through so many people at Amazon - managers, developers, product managers, PR people - and no one stopped to question whether this would actually be a good idea or not. https://twitter.com/...
Black Mirror is supposed to be a warning, not a pitch. https://twitter.com/...

Amazon has shown off an experimental Alexa feature that uses AI to mimic the voices of your dead relatives. The company says the feature “can't eliminate that pain of loss” but “can definitely make their memories last.” https://www.theverge.com/...

Thanks @washingtonpost @marialuisapaulr for chatting with me about the impersonation, hacking, ethics, disinformation, and harassment concerns this voice cloning tool bring up. https://twitter.com/...
There's a lot of very maladjusted people out there making very maladjusted tech https://twitter.com/...
this sounds creepy af, but if it's anything like the rest of Alexa then it probably won't work well 🤷 https://twitter.com/...
I think this is actually sort of cool. The dead grandma voice application seems weird but I am personally going to make Alexa talk like James Mason. https://twitter.com/...
Amazon can you just not tho https://twitter.com/...

Let us know when Amazon gets Alexa to mimic Paul Volcker's voice calling Jay Powell and Janet Yellen spineless and completely ignorant establishment tools .. then we'll pay attention https://twitter.com/...
no way this could be used for evil and nefarious purposes. none. https://twitter.com/...
Friends don't let friends have a functioning Alexa https://www.reuters.com/...
People think this is “creepy”, but I can't wait to have Gilbert Gottfried (why waste this power on a relative?!) talk to me https://www.cnbc.com/...
🤔 it sure will make memories last but not sure the way they think... divorce rate goes up 3x in households with Alexa 😂 https://twitter.com/...
Because who hasn't wanted their deceased loved one to set a kitchen timer for them in subservient-yet-cheerful deadpan? Alexa, play ‘neverending scream’ https://twitter.com/...
Alexa mimicking a dead family member sounds like a recipe for deep psychological damage. https://gizmodo.com/...
Soon you could opt to have a deceased loved one tell you stories, play music or simply turn on the lights - all from your Alexa device https://news.sky.com/...

Alexa will be able to mimic people's voices. But experts say the new feature is a Black Mirror-esque “slippery slope” that raises security and ethical issues. For ex: who has the rights to the data people leave to the ethers of the World Wide Web? https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...
Can it mimic the actors from a vintage gangster movie, to discourage burglars from breaking into your home https://twitter.com/...
Instagram begins testing tools to verify ages, starting with 17 or under, using either Yoti's facial analysis tech, photo IDs, or adults vouching for users
Introducing New Ways to Verify Age on Instagram
Instagram tests new age verification tools, including video selfies

Instagram to try out stronger age checks including video selfies
How Instagram will use selfie videos to detect user's age
Instagram is testing an AI-powered age estimating tool that you can try now
Instagram tests AI tool for age verification
Instagram cracks down on young users who lie about their age
Instagram will use video selfies to try and guess your age, ask people to vouch for you

Today @Meta introduce new ways for people in the US to verify their age on @instagram. The first option uses our privacy preserving Age Estimation technology. Learn more on: https://about.instagram.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

📣 Age Verification 📣 To help us provide people with experiences that are appropriate, it's critical for us to understand people's ages. Our approach to age verification now includes: - ID upload - Social vouching - Video selfie with @getyoti https://about.instagram.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

As a dad, offering safe, private experiences for teens across @meta is a personal priority. To do that well, we need to understand how old people are. Proud to partner with @getyoti to test new options for age verification on @instagram to help offer appropriate teen experiences. https://twitter.com/...

We're also announcing that we're starting to test new options for people to verify how old they are on Instagram, allowing us to further ensure that the right people get the right experiences. Glad we're partnering with @getyoti on this important work. https://about.fb.com/...

Starting today, we're testing new options for people on Instagram to verify their age. People can upload an ID, record a video selfie or ask mutual friends to verify. We're partnering with @getyoti, a company that specializes in online age verification. https://about.fb.com/...

Age verification is a complicated, industry-wide issue. But it's necessary in order to provide age-appropriate experiences for teens. These new age verification tools we're beginning to test today will allow us to do that. Grateful to @getyoti for their partnership on this effort https://twitter.com/...

It was if, hmm, there were new online child protection rules likely to come into force in California very soon that would require tech companies to do this. Nah, that has to be a coincidence, amirite? https://twitter.com/...
The system “is less accurate for female faces and people with darker skin” https://twitter.com/...

The people you choose for this process will get a confirmation request with 3 days to respond They will get options to specify your age: - <13 yrs - 13-17 - 18-20 - 21+ - Not sure All 3 people must choose same option for age verification to be approved https://techcrunch.com/...
Sources: the metaverse, not securing elections, is now Mark Zuckerberg's top priority; Meta has ~60 people focused on elections, down from 300 in 2020
Mark Zuckerberg envisions a billion people in the metaverse spending hundreds of dollars each
Mark Zuckerberg is more interested in the metaverse than election integrity, report says
Zuckerberg ditches election moderation in hot pursuit of metaverse revenue
Mike Shirkey fielded thousands of text messages asking him to overturn the 2020 election results after Trump publicly posted his phone number
@DavidClinchNews A delicious stat for sure. If only it were true. But it's not. As we told the Times. On the record.

Safeguarding elections is no longer Zuckerberg's top concern, said four Meta employees with knowledge of the situation. Further evidence that this concentration of power without sufficient regulation is not in the public interest. https://www.nytimes.com/...
🚨 after aiding an insurrection last cycle, @Meta has *cut its election team by 80%* as Zuck prioritizes the metaverse. https://www.nytimes.com/... https://twitter.com/...

⚠️@NYTimes reports @meta reduced election integrity teams before US elections, showing how committed @finkd is to our democracy — or that of other nations https://www.nytimes.com/... Will @Facebook “side” with far-right religious nationalists, or multiracial, pluralistic democracies? https://twitter.com/...
Instead, they meet with Nick Clegg, Meta's president of global affairs. https://www.nytimes.com/...

I'm not sure it's Mark Zuckerberg's job to “secure elections.” I think his job is to run a hugely successful company in the midst of a risky transition. At least I hope that's his job, speaking as a shareholder. https://www.nytimes.com/...

Con: “Well, this Facebook-powered autocratic propaganda machine that was exploited by an emergent fascist movement ended the Republic.” Pro: “The My Little Pony Club Metaverse space is LIT!” https://twitter.com/...

Very tired of tech reporters whitewashing Katie Harbath's image, putting her voice on par with racial justice leaders. For YEARS, Harbath was central in fighting to turn Facebook's policies into an engine of amplification for far-right politicians. https://www.nytimes.com/...
Wow. Facebook's election team is down 80% since 2020. https://www.nytimes.com/...

🙄Hm. So Zuck no longer cares about elections. Okaay. This won't just affect US midterms - it affects Kenya and many other nations. Electoral integrity for ±3 billion ppl shouldn't rely on the attention span of one billionaire. @thekhrc @MercyMutemi https://www.nytimes.com/...
Google details the commercial Hermit spyware, used in Kazakhstan and Italy, targeting Android and iOS; the iOS version contained two new zero-days
The curious tale of a fake Carrier.app
Spyware vendor targets users in Italy and Kazakhstan
Apple and Android phones hacked by Italian spyware, Google says
Google reveals sophisticated Italian spyware campaign targeting victims in Italy, Kazakhstan
Spyware Vendors Hawking Zero-Day Flaws Uncovered by Google
Excited to publish my writeup of a novel iOS in-the-wild exploit: The curious case of the fake Carrier .app: https://googleprojectzero.blogspot.com/ ...

NEW: meet Italian mercenary spyware vendor RCS Labs. Victims in: 🇮🇹Italy 🇰🇿Kazakhstan. One clever technique: cut victims data w/ISP complicity, then prompt them to load malicious app to ‘reconnect.’ 1/ By @benoitsevens & Clement Lecigne h/t @maddiestone https://blog.google/... https://twitter.com/...
Today, following on Lookout's report last week, Google TAG has published its own insight on the activities of Italian spyware vendor RCS Lab https://blog.google/... https://twitter.com/...

The Project Zero team also has a report on CVE-2021-30983, a novel iOS zero-day exploit abused by the company's tools (which also included tons of other exploits, see image) https://googleprojectzero.blogspot.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...
4/ There is also an abuse scandal involving RCS Labs, within 🇮🇹Italy. Yet another reminder that the 🇪🇺EU has a festering mercenary spyware problem. https://twitter.com/...

3/ @Lookout recently published a detailed investigation into RCS Lab, which has been a reseller for Hacking Team. Lookout does a nice job of highlighting the sketchy regimes they deal with. Recommended read: https://www.lookout.com/... https://twitter.com/...

2/ The inevitable question is “how does RCS Labs compare to...” Two datapoints: - This isn't zero click, this is a bunch of clicks & requires some user convincing - Look at these stale milk exploits... Yet obviously...it still works well enough that folks are paying for it. https://twitter.com/...

Google said Hermit's iOS spyware app used a zero-day flaw which was known at the time of discovery to be actively exploited. Hermit used Apple enterprise developer certificates to bypass the App Store, which Apple says it has now revoked. https://techcrunch.com/...
“A memory-corruption-based privilege escalation that side-stepped kernel mitigations by corrupting memory on a co-processor instead...” GPZ research https://googleprojectzero.blogspot.com/ ...

This follows Lookout's research into Hermit (https://t.co/...) which first attributed the spyware to RCS Lab. Google now has more details on the iOS version of the spyware, which abuses enterprise certificates to sideload outside of the app store. https://blog.google/...
Love me a little @Google Project Zero & TAG collabo.. New research out today about Italian spyware vendor, RCS Labs, capabilities and targeting of users in Italy & Kazakhstan. https://blog.google/...
“We assess, based on the extensive body of research and analysis by TAG and Project Zero, that the commercial spyware industry is thriving and growing at a significant rate.” https://blog.google/...

Nice to see a “short” 25-pager from @i41nbeer on a novel iOS exploit found in the wild. While both @Lookout and Google TAG weren't able to capture the exploits used for the Android version, a reminder that if you've found Android in the wild 0-day, we'd love to write about it! https://twitter.com/...
Check out Google's Threat Analysis Group's post which has more operational details: https://blog.google/...
New: Google is notifying Android users whose devices have been compromised by Hermit, a government-grade spyware used against victims in Italy and Kazakhstan. https://techcrunch.com/...
An in-depth look at the LUNA and UST fallout, as Do Kwon says he has been “devastated” but has “great confidence in our ability to build back even stronger”
Terra's Do Kwon: ‘There Is a Difference Between Failing and Running a Fraud’
Do Kwon's Crypto Empire Fell in a $40 Billion Crash.  He's Got a New Coin for You.

Do Kwon has ‘great confidence’ in rebuilding Terra blockchain: WSJ

“I don't understand why anybody in their right mind would want to invest in Luna 2 after watching Luna 1 blow up so dramatically,” said Mati Greenspan, founder of crypto research firm Quantum Economics. https://www.wsj.com/...

On claims that he ran a scam: “I made confident bets and made confident statements on behalf of UST because I believed in its resilience and its value proposition... I've since lost these bets, but my actions 100% match my words.” (4/7)

Earlier this year analysts said Kwon was a billionaire, based on his Luna holdings. Kwon said that was probably the case, though he had “never really counted”—and that he lost nearly all his net worth in the crash. “This doesn't bother me... I live a fairly frugal life.” (3/7)

Like 3AC, Do Kwon has only been interviewed by WSJ, he said: I have great confidence in our ability to build back even stronger than we once were. Asked about the tweets, Mr. Kwon told the Journal: “Do I regret some of the things I said in the past? Yes.” https://www.wsj.com/...

He's facing angry investors and government investigations. But that's not stopping Do Kwon from attempting a comeback. “I have great confidence in our ability to build back even stronger than we once were,” he told the WSJ in an interview. (1/7) https://www.wsj.com/...

Another fascinating piece by @aosipovich giving us an insight into Do Kwon's current thought process & cover-up methods. https://wsj.com/... (Non-paywall: https://archive.ph/HNl1T) Looks like the WSJ were able to speak to and verify the Anchor team source as well. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

one of the really interesting parts of crypto is you can be weird and sketchy AND be the market leader. That's sorta mind blowing https://www.bloomberg.com/... https://twitter.com/...

The other day, Binance's CEO tweeted a (joking?) photoshop job of a fake office. Found this amusing cause a few weeks earlier I'd been invited to see the Binance office in Dubai and found...not exactly an office https://www.bloomberg.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Zhao said “people only go to the offices a couple of days a week.”...Inside an expansive space with an amazing view of central Dubai “were bare concrete floors, exposed ducts, drywall—and nothing else...” Interesting on Binance by @justinaknope @chafkin https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Zhao says he's a fan of leaderless movements that coalesce around the immutable logic of blockchain. But when it comes to Binance, it's CZ alone who controls it. (h/t @justinaknope & @chafkin) https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Went to dubai to write about binance, and to try to understand whether this is the crazy future of finance, or just the worlds biggest unlicensed casino. New @BW cover story with @justinaknope https://www.bloomberg.com/...
hedgefund boys own yatchs cz owns a minivan CHAD https://www.bloomberg.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Bloomberg cover pieces like this usually are a top indicator (see Shopify BBG piece). https://www.bloomberg.com/...

For a subculture besotted with Lambos, crypto bros' projected nonchalance about personal wealth seems rather disingenuous. Exhibit A: Crypto's richest man says: “I don't really care much about money.” https://www.bloomberg.com/... @justinaknope @chafkin @crypto

Singapore's fintech policy chief says the country will be “brutal and unrelentingly hard” on “any market bad behaviour” in the crypto industry
Singapore's watchdog vows to be ‘brutal and unrelentingly hard’ on the crypto sector's bad actors

Singapore regulator will be ‘brutal’ on bad behavior in crypto, FT reports
Singapore: How Disneyland with a Death Penalty Approaches Crypto

FT：Singapore will be “brutal and unrelentingly hard” on bad behaviour in the crypto industry, according to its fintech policy chief, marking a stark shift in rhetoric after years of the city-state courting the sector. https://www.ft.com/...

Singapore is vowing to be “brutal and unrelentingly hard” on crypto. “We have been called out by many cryptocurrencies for not being friendly. My response has been: friendly for what? Friendly for a real economy or friendly for some unreal economy?” https://www.ft.com/...

“We have been called out by many cryptocurrencies for not being friendly. My response has been: friendly for what? Friendly for a real economy or friendly for some unreal economy?” Singapore regulator vows to be ‘unrelentingly hard’ on crypto https://www.ft.com/...

Sopnendu Mohanty, chief fintech officer at the Monetary Authority of Singapore, to the @FT: “We have no tolerance for any market bad behaviour. If somebody has done a bad thing, we are brutal and unrelentingly hard.” https://www.ft.com/...

Singapore is expected to launch its central bank digital currency within three years, FT reports, quoting Sopnendu Mohanty, the chief fintech officer at MAS. He says Singapore has “no tolerance” for bad behaviour in the crypto industry. https://www.ft.com/...

All the good vibes on the #ADPPA shot down in one swoop via key reporting from @viaCristiano. Cantwell's opposition was known to a degree, but now it is clear as day that we're going to have a major hang-up in the Senate https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

And it's not just Cantwell. Blumenthal, Wyden and Schatz have all expressed serious reservations. More here: https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...
Say it again: a federal privacy bill will not pass this year. The proposal still represents progress, but everybody calibrate your expectations. https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

🚨 NEWS: Sen Cantwell throws cold water on proposed #ADPPA. “Chuck Schumer has already said there's no way they're bringing that bill up in the Senate, so they can come back to the table on something strong,” https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...
The bill is too weak https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...
A hacktivist claims DALL-E-like Craiyon, formerly DALL-E mini, generated portraits of brown-skinned women in saris almost every time he ran a blank request

Earlier in the week, @NilChristopher emailed me with a fascinating mystery: DALL-E Mini repeatedly produces images of women in saris when prompted with a blank input. He did some digging to find out why, but the mystery persists. A really nice piece: https://restofworld.org/...

Why is viral AI image generator DALL.E mini obsessed with brown women in saris? I set out to solve this mystery for @restofworld which even its creator couldn't explain. Read to find out. https://restofworld.org/...

Can't make this up. Our future is trying to explain why an AI system came up with something really specific when it wasn't expected to. And pointing fingers at training data! Via @NilChristopher “DALL·E mini has a mysterious obsession with women in saris” https://restofworld.org/... https://twitter.com/...

The whimsical image generator everyone is suddenly obsessed with has a “glitch,” as this story calls it, that even its creator can't explain. Great illustration by ⁦@restofworld⁩ of how opaque AI can be. & companies use it for big customer decisions https://restofworld.org/...
Sari not sorry — DALL·E mini, maybe https://restofworld.org/...

In year 1, our private & independent search engine passed some amazing milestones: - 2.5B total queries - A high of 14.1M queries in a day - 5B queries annualized (from monthly totals) Through features like Goggles, we'll continue to give you new ways to fight Big Tech. https://twitter.com/...

Brave Search is now one year old and officially out of beta! We're celebrating by giving you even more control over your searches. Our new feature, Goggles, allows you to create and share your own sets of rules and filters for search results. https://brave.com/...

Unlike Google + Bing, Brave Search doesn't manipulate its algorithm to bias or down-rank results. However, all search engines have some level of intrinsic bias. Goggles gives you the power to search without these limits. Try Goggles (in beta) today! https://search.brave.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Goggles show real search innovation (Big Tech in monopoly phase, tying their businesses together, is the opposite of innovation). A Goggle is a URL-addressed file of adblock filter syntax for re-ranking the index to suit your or your community's needs. https://github.com/... https://twitter.com/...
